theme: Give the office a memory — the best dashboards reward repeat visitors
visual_observations: Review #46 visual notes still current. Whiteboard label fix from #46 already shipped (code confirms wb-progress-label exists). DESK_SCREEN_SVG covers 5 workers. Vibe transitions still only show in speech bubbles, not activity log. Mobile hover cards still unreachable on touch. Previous cycle shipped: hover card time-on-task, slow-day whiteboard, 20 transition quotes, polling-paused pill, plant toast.

ideas.title: Log Vibe Transitions in the Activity Feed
ideas.description: Carried from review #46 — still unshipped. The activity log records hire/complete/error/system events but vibe transitions are invisible there. They only appear in Bloberto's speech bubbles which vanish in 4s. When a vibe transition fires (prevKey !== null && prevKey !== teamVibe.key in App.jsx), push a synthetic entry to activityLog: { type: 'system', worker: null, message: '🌡️ Vibe: prevLabel → newLabel', timestamp: now }. WHY: The team's emotional arc across the day IS the story. The activity log is the permanent record — vibe shifts belong there.

ideas.title: Mobile Tap-to-Pin Worker Hover Cards
ideas.description: Carried from review #46 — still unshipped. On mobile, hover cards with name/role/task/status/on-task-time are unreachable (onMouseEnter never fires on touch). Add pinnedCard state to Character component. On click, if window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches, toggle pinnedCard instead of firing handleClick. Show hover card when hovered || pinnedCard. Close on outside tap. WHY: Mobile is the screen most likely to be propped up near a coffee machine. Half the app's meaning vanishes on touch devices.

ideas.title: Desk Lamp Color Matches Worker Role
ideas.description: The desk lamp SVG (Office.jsx) always glows yellow (#fbbf24) regardless of who sits there. When isWorking, tint the lamp ellipse fill to ROLE_COLORS[occ.worker.role] with 60% opacity overlay. WHY: Subtle role-color reinforcement without adding clutter. Walking into an office and seeing different colored desk lamps instantly tells you who's where.

ideas.title: Fallback Desk Screen for Unlisted Workers
ideas.description: Carried from review #46. DESK_SCREEN_SVG covers 5 IDs. Any new hire renders a blank black monitor. Add a 'default' entry using ROLE_COLORS as line fill. WHY: A dead-black screen next to five rich monitors looks like a bug.

ideas.title: Daily Stats Ticker on the Whiteboard
ideas.description: Below the vibe text and progress bar, show a rotating stat every 10s: 'Tasks shipped today: N' → 'Team uptime: Xh' → 'Vibe changes: N'. Pull from activityLog entries filtered to today. WHY: The whiteboard has real estate and is the focal point of the office. A slowly rotating stat ticker gives repeat visitors something new to glance at.

ideas.title: Visitor Counter — You Are Not Alone
ideas.description: Add a subtle 'N people watching the office right now' indicator using a free analytics ping (e.g., a simple counter service or localStorage-simulated for demo). Even if faked with a plausible random number, it creates social proof and makes the experience feel communal. WHY: Knowing others are watching transforms a dashboard into a shared experience.
